definition: Nibbāna understood as present or immediate in this very life.
notes: Keep the rendering tied to early-text liberation usage and avoid overloading the term with later metaphysical systematization. The phrase belongs in the nibbāna family and should not be rewritten into generic worldly happiness or premature-finality language.

example_phrases.notes: Compound governing immediate-life nibbāna language without collapsing into worldly comfort or final nibbāna at death.
